commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f7a3976b4d0944cdd404ddd12de65b012dbfc545)
commands.review_summary:
The PR guards browser tab cleanup in auto-reply session rollover and gateway `sessions.reset` when `browser.enabled` is false, with regression tests for both paths.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +52. Total +57 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main calls the browser maintenance facade from reset and rollover paths, and the facade loads the bundled browser surface for non-empty session keys. I did not run a live repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor real behavior proof and maintainer/code review for the disabled-browser contract; automation should not take over while proof from the contributor's setup is missing.

target.title: Skip browser cleanup when browser is disabled
target.body:
## Summary
- skip browser tab cleanup during auto-reply rollover when browser support is explicitly disabled
- skip the same browser cleanup path for gateway sessions.reset with browser.enabled=false
- add coverage for disabled-browser rollover and sessions.reset behavior

## Why
When browser support is disabled, session reset can still call the browser maintenance facade. That facade may cold-load the bundled browser surface even though there are no browser tabs to clean up, adding avoidable latency and memory pressure to non-browser runtimes.

## Validation
- pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --ignore-scripts
- pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-cleanup.test.ts --config vitest.config.ts
target.author: poison
